2605.30051.title: Who Am I? History-Aware Profiles for Student Simulation in Tutoring Dialogues
2605.30051.paper_summary: A key part of developing large language model (LLM)-powered, automated tutoring tools is student simulation, i.e., using LLMs to role-play as students, which can facilitate tutor model evaluation and training. Existing work mostly focuses on within-dialogue simulation, which lacks context on student knowledge and behavior, partly due to not grounding in past student question-answering or dialogue interactions. In this work, we introduce the task of history-conditioned student simulation, where the goal is to accurately predict student dialogue turns by leveraging information in the student's learning history. We propose a two-component framework in which a profile generator summarizes a student's history and a simulator predicts student turns conditioned on the resulting profile. We train both components with reinforcement learning (RL), yielding profiles optimized for faithful student simulation. We evaluate our method and baselines on the first-of-its-kind real-world dataset of student dialogues and question responses that we collect from a math learning platform. Extensive experiments show that our method significantly outperforms baselines, and demonstrate the importance of history, profiles, and RL training.
2605.30051.paper_summary_zh: 開發大型語言模型（LLM）驅動的自動輔導工具的一個關鍵部分是學生模擬，即使用LLM進行角色扮演，以模擬學生，這可以促進輔導模型的評估和訓練。現有的研究主要集中在對話內模擬，這缺乏學生知識和行為的背景，部分原因是沒有基於過去學生的問答或對話互動。 在本研究中，我們引入了歷史條件學生模擬的任務，其目標是利用學生的學習歷史中的信息準確預測學生的對話回合。我們提出了一個由兩個組件組成的框架，其中個人檔案生成器總結學生的歷史，而模擬器則根據生成的個人檔案預測學生的回合。我們使用強化學習（RL）訓練這兩個組件，產生優化的個人檔案，以實現真實的學生模擬。我們在從數學學習平台收集的首個真實世界學生對話和問題回應數據集上評估我們的方法和基準。大量實驗表明，我們的方法顯著優於基準，並展示了歷史、個人檔案和RL訓練的重要性。
2605.30051.author: Zhangqi Duan et.al.
2605.30051.authors: Zhangqi Duan, Shuyan Huang, Alexander Scarlatos, Jaewook Lee, Simon Woodhead, Andrew Lan
